blocks.content: His mom was making dinner when he got home. The kitchen smelled like the thing she made on Fridays — pasta, garlic, the radio low — and Kit dropped his backpack at the door and stood in the kitchen doorway for a second.

She looked at him over her shoulder with the expression he'd been getting his whole life — the one that said she could see more than he was showing, and had decided to let him show it at his own pace.
He set the table without being asked, which was something he only did when he was in a particular kind of mood, and his mom noted it and didn't comment, because she was good at that.

After dinner he went upstairs and opened AIM. The buddy list loaded. Daisy was online, her status changed from this morning — just the default now, nothing. Brooke was online too, her icon showing active.
He clicked on his own screen name and changed his away message. Sat back. Looked at it.
It said: i'll always come find you.
Across town, a buddy list refreshed. A status changed. Someone read it and didn't respond.
